pytorch.reason: load_bearing via PyG: PyG MessagePassing requires torch tensors for edge_index and node feature matrices; all feature propagation runs through torch.Tensor operations inside the MessagePassing layer

pyg.reason: load_bearing: PyG MessagePassing propagates similarity scores across the LSH-constructed graph; node features after MessagePassing differ from initial features, demonstrating genuine graph computation that depends on the edge structure supplied by datasketch LSH

z3.reason: not needed -- LSH graph construction and MessagePassing do not require formal constraint encoding or SAT/SMT reasoning

cvc5.reason: not needed -- no quantifier reasoning required for similarity graph

sympy.reason: not needed -- Jaccard similarity and MinHash are numeric; no symbolic algebra required

clifford.reason: not needed -- node features are bag-of-tokens vectors, not Clifford algebra elements

geomstats.reason: not needed -- graph topology is flat; no Riemannian manifold structure involved

e3nn.reason: not needed -- no SO(3) equivariance in the token similarity graph

rustworkx.reason: not needed -- graph is passed directly to PyG as edge_index tensor; no separate graph library needed for this sim

xgi.reason: not needed -- LSH graph has pairwise edges only, no hyperedges

toponetx.reason: not needed -- no cell complex structure; sim uses simple graph message passing on LSH edges

gudhi.reason: not needed -- no persistent homology; topology of the sim graph is captured by LSH adjacency

positive.skipped: datasketch or torch or pyg not available

negative.skipped: datasketch or torch or pyg not available
